market.advice: Pre-open with the uptrend technically intact but two-tier: SPY $748.3 holds above its 50-day (743.6) and is only -1.2% off its high with breadth healthy at 70% above the 50-day, yet QQQ ($704.8) has slipped back below ITS 50-day and VIX is quietly rising (18.1 vs 17.1 five-day). The tape's message is ROTATION, and it's now emphatic: money is leaving mega-cap tech and semis (NVDA, AMD and AMAT all carry a TradingView Sell; MSFT/GOOGL soft) and pouring into financials & insurers, REITs, healthcare/managed-care, energy and defensive staples/industrials — exactly the groups filling the top of today's board. A fresh leg of Middle East tension (pump prices back over $4, yields ticking up) is a tailwind for the energy and financials leadership. Into the open: buy the leadership on clean, un-extended setups (FTNT, AIZ, JPM, UNH, MNST, KIM), keep normal position sizing while the state stays green, don't chase names already overbought (PGNY, CSX, SPGI, XOM all closed hot), and honor stops on the lagging tech/semi complex rather than averaging down. Educational, not financial advice.

picks.thesis: Top scorer on the board and the cleanest large-cap tech setup that isn't a semi: Fortinet sits just -2.8% off its high with RSI a moderate 56, a tight 5-contraction VCP base (3.5%) and huge 3-month momentum (+96%). Cybersecurity is a rare tech pocket the rotation is rewarding, and TradingView independently rates it Buy — confirmation of the breakout.
strengths: Tightest base among the tech leaders (5 contractions, 3.5%)
RSI only 56 — room to run, not extended
risks: Earnings Jul 30 (~10 days out) — event risk builds into the entry
A broad tech drawdown would drag it even though fundamentals lead

picks.thesis: Assurant is the textbook rotation name: a specialty insurer -1.2% off its high on a very tight 3-contraction VCP (2.1%), RSI 67, with explosive EPS growth (+91%). Insurance is squarely in the leadership pocket and TradingView agrees with a Buy.
strengths: Very tight VCP base (2.1%) — low-risk entry
Defensive-growth insurer in the leading group
risks: Earnings Aug 5 (~16 days) approaching
Catastrophe-loss exposure is the sector's perennial wildcard

picks.thesis: The megabank leader in a tape rotating hard into financials: JPMorgan is -1.0% off its high with a clean 5-contraction VCP (2.5%), RSI 65, EPS +47% and revenue +30%. Q2 already reported so there is no near-term earnings landmine. TradingView rates it Buy.
strengths: Best-in-class bank at a new-high area, low base risk
risks: A sharp rate move against the yield-curve trade
Group leadership can rotate quickly if breadth narrows

picks.thesis: Citizens Financial gives regional-bank leverage to the rotation: -2.9% off its high, RSI 56 (un-extended), EPS +41%, and earnings already reported. TradingView Buy backs the setup.
strengths: Un-extended RSI (56) with room to run
risks: Base is a touch looser (4.9%) than the tightest names
Credit/CRE headlines can hit regionals disproportionately

picks.thesis: Aflac is one of the few names where TradingView independently rates it STRONG BUY — double confirmation. A supplemental-insurance compounder on a tight 3-contraction VCP (2.4%), RSI 64, right in the leading insurance group.
risks: Yen/FX translation exposure (large Japan book)
Earnings Aug 7 (~18 days)

picks.thesis: Travelers pairs a fresh-high posture (-1.0% off high) with TradingView's STRONG BUY. P&C insurer with pricing power, EPS +57%, and earnings already reported — clean into a rotation that favors insurers.
risks: RSI 69 is warm — not overbought but watch chasing
Catastrophe-loss quarters can whipsaw the group

picks.thesis: Goldman Sachs has pulled back a healthy -6.2% off its high into a 4-contraction VCP (2.4%) with RSI 59 — a lower-risk re-entry than the names pinned at new highs. EPS +92%, revenue +42%, earnings already reported, TradingView Buy. Widely held on this platform.
risks: Highest-priced name here — size positions accordingly
Capital-markets revenue is cyclical if deal flow cools

picks.thesis: Kimco Realty is the REIT leadership expression: printing a fresh 52-week high yet with RSI only 55 (not extended), a 3-contraction VCP and EPS +29%. Rate-sensitive REITs are catching the rotation bid; TradingView rates it Buy.
strengths: Grocery-anchored retail REIT, defensive cash flow
risks: A back-up in yields would pressure REITs broadly
Earnings Aug 4 (~15 days)

picks.thesis: Simon Property Group, the premium-mall REIT, is at a fresh high with RSI a calm 53 and revenue +19%. Best-of-breed REIT riding the rate-sensitive rotation; TradingView Buy. Held on the platform.
strengths: Fresh high, RSI only 53 — lots of runway
risks: Yield spikes hit REIT multiples
Earnings Aug 11 (~22 days)

picks.thesis: UnitedHealth is the cleanest managed-care recovery: -2.4% off its high, RSI a low 51 (plenty of room), EPS +61%, on a 3-contraction VCP (2.9%). TradingView independently rates it STRONG BUY — double confirmation into the healthcare rotation.
risks: Headline/regulatory risk is elevated for the group
A large-cap health name — index moves matter

picks.thesis: CVS Health is right at its high (-0.1%) with RSI 64 and EPS +63% on a tight 4-contraction VCP (2.6%). The healthcare-services turnaround is being rewarded by the rotation; TradingView rates it Buy.
strengths: Tight base (2.6%) at a new-high area
risks: Earnings Aug 5 (~16 days)
PBM/regulatory scrutiny is an overhang

picks.thesis: Howmet Aerospace is the industrials leader: -2.2% off its high, RSI 59, a tight 5-contraction VCP (2.5%), EPS +71%. Aero/defense demand plus the industrials rotation; TradingView Buy. Held on the platform.
strengths: EPS +71% YoY — real aerospace cycle demand
risks: Earnings Aug 6 (~17 days)
Aero supply-chain hiccups are the sector risk

picks.thesis: EOG Resources is a top-tier E&P catching a real fundamental tailwind: renewed Middle East tension has pump prices back over $4 and oil bid. -5.9% off its high on a deep 6-contraction VCP (2.9%), RSI 63, EPS +40%. TradingView Buy.
strengths: Oil tailwind from Middle East escalation
risks: Oil is the swing factor — a ceasefire could cap the move
Earnings Aug 5 (~16 days)

picks.thesis: Diamondback Energy is the Permian pure-play with TradingView's STRONG BUY. -8.7% off its high (constructive base) on a tight 5-contraction VCP (2.5%), RSI 64, riding the same oil tailwind as EOG.
risks: Reported EPS distorted by one-offs — lean on the cash story
Fully oil-price dependent
Earnings Aug 4 (~15 days)

picks.thesis: DaVita is a co-top scorer: the dialysis leader is essentially at its high (-0.3%) on a tight 3-contraction VCP (2.6%), EPS +44%, cheap at ~14x. Defensive healthcare with buyback-driven EPS — exactly what the rotation wants. TradingView Buy.
strengths: EPS +44% and ~14x forward — cheap defensive growth
risks: RSI 71 is warm — avoid chasing far above entry
Reimbursement/policy risk is the structural overhang
Earnings Aug 6 (~17 days)

picks.thesis: Monster Beverage is the cleanest staples-growth setup: RSI only 49 (freshly reset, lots of room), -2.6% off its high on a tight 3-contraction VCP (2.6%), EPS +29% / revenue +27%. Asset-light, net-cash, and squarely defensive-growth. TradingView Buy.
risks: Fwd P/E ~38 leaves little room for a growth miss
Earnings Aug 7 (~18 days)

picks.thesis: Starbucks is a consumer turnaround at a new-high area: -2.2% off its high, RSI 55, a tight 4-contraction VCP (2.4%), EPS +33%. The 'back to Starbucks' operational reset is showing up in the tape. TradingView Buy.
risks: Earnings Jul 30 (~10 days) — the key turnaround check-in
China same-store trends remain a swing factor

picks.thesis: CrowdStrike is the second cybersecurity leader the rotation still rewards: -2.9% off its high, RSI 63, +89% 3-month momentum, revenue +26%. TradingView Buy. The one caveat is valuation — fwd P/E ~131 — so this is a momentum-leader position, not a value one.
risks: Fwd P/E ~131 — extremely rich; multiple-compression risk
Base is looser (6.1%) than the tightest names

picks.thesis: Progyny scores near the top (fertility-benefits, EPS +71%) and TradingView rates it Buy, but RSI 73 and +69% 3-month momentum make it extended here — a strong story to buy on a pullback into the base rather than chase pre-open.
risks: RSI 73 — overbought, poor risk/reward chasing
Earnings Aug 7; small-cap volatility

picks.thesis: Dexcom scores well (EPS +92%, RSI 63, TradingView Buy) but is still -14.9% below its high, rebuilding a wide 6-contraction base — a recovery, not a fresh breakout. Watch for it to clear the base before committing.
risks: -14.9% off high — not yet a confirmed breakout
Earnings Jul 31 (~11 days) is a binary near-term event

picks.thesis: S&P Global is a high-quality compounder and scores 90, but RSI 75 and -15.6% off its high signal a name that's bounced hard within a broken structure — and earnings land Jul 28 (~8 days). Prefer to watch through the print.
risks: RSI 75 — extended into an earnings event (Jul 28)
-15.6% off high — still repairing structure

picks.thesis: Amazon still scores 90 (EPS +75%, TradingView Buy) but is -9.4% off its high with 3-month momentum flat — a mega-cap consolidating while the rotation flows elsewhere. Held on the platform; a hold/accumulate-on-strength, not a fresh chase, with earnings Jul 31.
risks: Flat momentum while money rotates out of mega-cap
Earnings Jul 31 (~11 days)

picks.thesis: DTE Energy screens as a 90 on the quant model (tight utility base), but here's the tension: TradingView rates it only NEUTRAL and RSI is a weak 40 with flat momentum — the technicals disagree with the score. Held on the platform; treat as a defensive hold and watch for the base to firm before adding. Earnings Jul 28.
strengths: Regulated-utility stability, defensive in a rotation
risks: TradingView NEUTRAL and RSI 40 — model and technicals disagree
Earnings Jul 28 (~8 days); rate-sensitive

picks.thesis: Vita Coco scores 88 with strong momentum (+58% 3-month, revenue +37%, TradingView Buy), but it reports earnings in just ~3 days (Jul 23) and sits -10.9% off its high — too much event risk to initiate pre-open. Held on the platform; watch the print.
risks: Earnings Jul 23 (~3 days) — major binary event
-10.9% off high; small-cap gap risk

picks.thesis: Fair Isaac is a wide-moat compounder (EPS +69%, revenue +39%, TradingView Buy) but sits -33.2% below its high in a deep base — a repair, not a breakout — with earnings Jul 30. Held on the platform; watch for a base clear rather than catching the falling knife.
risks: -33.2% off high — deep, unproven base
Earnings Jul 30 (~10 days); high absolute price

picks.thesis: Snowflake is one of the few software names with real momentum (+80% 3-month, revenue +34%, TradingView Buy) and sits just -3.2% off its high, but RSI 69 and only a 2-contraction base make it extended. Held on the platform; a hold, add on a pullback into the base.
risks: RSI 69 + only 2 contractions — extended, loose base
No GAAP profits yet; rich multiple

picks.thesis: NetApp scores 86 (EPS +23%, +52% 3-month momentum, TradingView Buy) but is -10.5% off its high on a loose 2-contraction base — constructive recovery, not yet a clean breakout. Held on the platform; watch it clear the high before adding.
risks: -10.5% off high on a loose base (8.5%)
Enterprise-hardware cyclicality

picks.thesis: Palantir has explosive fundamentals (EPS +325%, revenue +85%) but here's the tension: it's -35% off its high with NEGATIVE 3-month momentum and TradingView rates it only NEUTRAL — the chart and the second opinion both say wait. Held on the platform; hold, do not add until it stabilizes.
risks: -35% off high with negative momentum — broken chart
TradingView NEUTRAL; extreme valuation amplifies downside

picks.thesis: Liquidia scores 92 on a fresh Yutrepia (pulmonary-hypertension) launch ramp (TradingView Buy), but it's up +114% in three months on a wide base — a parabolic small-cap biotech, not a low-risk entry. Held on the platform; hold and trail a stop rather than adding here.
strengths: Commercial launch inflection, revenue ramping fast
risks: +114% in 3 months — parabolic and extended
Small-cap biotech: binary catalyst/legal risk; earnings Aug 12

picks.thesis: NEW LISTING — SpaceX, the dominant launch + Starlink franchise, is a compelling long-term story, but be honest about the chart: as a recent IPO it has sold off hard post-listing (-41.6% off its high, RSI a deeply oversold 21, negative 3-month momentum) and TradingView now rates it Sell. The post-IPO valuation reset is playing out. Held on the platform; treat as a thesis-driven, speculative position — the franchise is elite, but the tape is broken, so wait for stabilization / a real base rather than catching the falling knife.
strengths: Category-defining launch + Starlink franchise (the long-term thesis)
Deeply oversold (RSI 21) — capable of a sharp mean-reversion bounce
risks: -41.6% off high, negative momentum, TradingView Sell — trend is down
Newly public: lock-up expiry and valuation-reset volatility; speculative
